q.en: According to the book, which two men decided to build the Empire State Building?
q.zh: 根据书中内容，是哪两个人决定建造帝国大厦的？

options.en: Hitler and Napoleon
Martin Blake and Shel Dorf
options.zh: 马丁·布莱克和谢尔·多尔夫

options.en: George Washington and Thomas Jefferson according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 乔治·华盛顿和托马斯·杰斐逊（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Alfred E. Smith and John J. Raskob
options.zh: 阿尔弗雷德·E·史密斯和约翰·J·拉斯科布

explain.en: The book says Alfred E. Smith and John J. Raskob, two friends who grew up in poor immigrant families, made a winning team and decided to build the famous skyscraper.
explain.zh: 书中说阿尔弗雷德·E·史密斯和约翰·J·拉斯科布两位在贫困移民家庭长大的朋友组成了绝佳搭档，决定建造这座著名的摩天大楼。

q.en: Before the Empire State Building was constructed, what stood on the site at Fifth Avenue and 34th Street?
q.zh: 在帝国大厦建成之前，第五大道与34街交汇处的原址上是什么建筑？

options.en: A public park
The Statue of Liberty
A small train station according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 一座小火车站（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: The luxurious Waldorf-Astoria Hotel
options.zh: 豪华的华尔道夫-阿斯托里亚酒店

explain.en: The book explains Smith and Raskob wanted to build where Fifth Avenue and 34th Street crossed, but the Waldorf-Astoria Hotel was already there, so they bought and demolished it.
explain.zh: 书中说史密斯和拉斯科布想在第五大道与34街交叉处建造，但华尔道夫-阿斯托里亚酒店已在那里，于是他们买下并拆除了它。

q.en: On what date was the Empire State Building officially opened?
q.zh: 帝国大厦是在哪一天正式开放的？

options.en: September 3, 1783
July 4, 1776
June 6, 1944

explain.en: The book states that in just over a year, on May 1, 1931, the Empire State Building was officially opened, with President Hoover, Smith, and Governor Roosevelt taking part.
explain.zh: 书中说仅仅一年多后，即1931年5月1日，帝国大厦正式开放，胡佛总统、史密斯和罗斯福州长等参加了开幕活动。

q.en: The Empire State Building was the world's tallest skyscraper when completed in 1931. According to the book, which building took over that title in 1971?
q.zh: 帝国大厦在1931年建成时是世界最高的摩天大楼。根据书中内容，哪座建筑在1971年取而代之成为最高？

options.en: The Waldorf-Astoria
options.zh: 华尔道夫-阿斯托里亚酒店

options.en: The Eiffel Tower
The World Trade Center
The Chrysler Building

explain.en: The book says the Empire State Building held the title longer than any other building, until the World Trade Center took over as the tallest in 1971.
explain.zh: 书中说帝国大厦保持这一称号的时间比任何建筑都长，直到1971年世界贸易中心成为最高建筑。

q.en: The author writes that the steel grid rose 'at the breakneck rate of four-and-one-half floors per week.' What does this word choice help show?
q.zh: 作者写道钢架以'每周四层半的惊人速度'上升。这样的措辞有助于说明什么？

options.en: That the building was never finished. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 这座建筑从未完工。（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: How remarkably fast the construction proceeded.
options.zh: 工程建设速度快得惊人。

options.en: That workers were paid very little.
That the hotel was easy to demolish.

explain.en: The phrase 'breakneck rate' is figurative language the author uses to emphasize the unusually fast pace of the skyscraper's construction.
explain.zh: 作者用'惊人速度'这一比喻性语言，强调摩天大楼建设速度快得不同寻常。

q.en: The book says one cart on the floor tracks 'could hold as many supplies as eight wheelbarrows.' What does this comparison help the reader understand?
q.zh: 书中说楼层轨道上的一个小推车'能装下相当于八个独轮车的物资'。这一对比帮助读者理解什么？

options.en: How much more efficient the cart-and-track system was than wheelbarrows.
options.zh: 小车加轨道系统比独轮车高效得多。

options.en: That wheelbarrows were banned on site. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 工地上禁止使用独轮车。（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: That eight men pushed each cart.
That the building had eight floors.

explain.en: The author uses a comparison to a familiar object (the wheelbarrow) so readers can grasp the efficiency gain from the track-and-cart innovation.
explain.zh: 作者用读者熟悉的独轮车作对比，让人理解轨道小车这一创新带来的效率提升。

q.en: The book describes how the building's spire was first meant to be a mooring mast for dirigibles. What happened, and what became of the mast in 1950?
q.zh: 书中描述大厦尖顶最初被设计为飞艇的系泊桅杆。结果如何？1950年这个桅杆又派上了什么用场？

options.en: It was removed in 1950 because it was unsafe.
Winds were too strong for dirigibles to dock, and in 1950 the mast became an antenna base for television.
options.zh: 风太大飞艇无法停靠，1950年桅杆成为电视天线基座。

options.en: Dirigibles docked daily until 1950, when it became a restaurant. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 飞艇每天停靠，直到1950年改成餐厅。（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: It was used for King Kong to climb in 1950.

explain.en: The book says when dirigibles approached, winds were too strong to dock; it was not until 1950 that the mast found a use as a television antenna base.
explain.zh: 书中说飞艇靠近时风太大无法停靠；直到1950年桅杆才被用作电视天线基座。

q.en: The book connects the building's construction to the Great Depression. What happened in the wider economy as the building rose?
q.zh: 书中将大厦的建造与大萧条联系起来。在大厦建设期间，更广阔的经济发生了什么？

options.en: The economy boomed and everyone got rich.
The stock market crashed in 1929 and about one-fourth of American workers were unemployed within a year, yet the building provided good-pay work.
options.zh: 1929年股市崩盘，一年内约四分之一美国工人失业，但大厦提供了待遇优厚的岗位。

options.en: A war began and stopped all construction. according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book as mentioned in a later chapter
options.zh: 战争爆发，所有工程停工。（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）（正如后文某一章提到的）

options.en: The hotel business grew even larger.

explain.en: The book says as workers razed the hotel, the stock market crashed and led to the Great Depression; within a year almost one-fourth of American workers were unemployed, but well-paid work continued at the building.
explain.zh: 书中说工人们拆除酒店时股市崩盘并引发大萧条；一年内近四分之一美国工人失业，但大厦工程仍提供待遇优厚的岗位。

q.en: The author describes the dangerous work of the steelworkers called 'sky boys' and notes the site had its own hospital. Why include these details?
q.zh: 作者描述了被称为'sky boys'的钢铁工人的危险工作，并提到工地设有自己的医院。为何加入这些细节？

options.en: To highlight the bravery of the workers and the human effort behind the building.
options.zh: 凸显工人的勇敢以及建造这座建筑背后的人力付出。

options.en: To prove the building was short.
To describe the hotel's furniture.
To explain how elevators were invented. according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 解释电梯是如何发明的。（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: By detailing the risky labor and on-site medical care, the author shows readers the real human cost and effort involved in erecting the skyscraper.
explain.zh: 通过描写危险的劳动和现场医疗，作者向读者展示建造摩天大楼真实的人力代价与付出。

q.en: What is the author's main purpose in writing Empire State Building?
q.zh: 作者写《帝国大厦》的主要目的是什么？

options.en: To describe life in the 1800s.
To teach readers how to ski.
To explain the history, construction, and landmark significance of an iconic New York building.
options.zh: 介绍这座纽约标志性建筑的历史、建造过程及其地标意义。

options.en: To argue that hotels should never be torn down. according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 主张永远不应拆除酒店。（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: The book is an informational text about a world landmark, meant to teach its history, how it was built, and why it matters.
explain.zh: 这是一本关于世界地标的资讯性读物，旨在介绍其历史、建造方式及重要意义。

prompt.en: Imagine you are a 'sky boy' working on the Empire State Building in 1930. What would worry you most about the job, and what would make you proud? Connect your answer to the risks the book describes.
prompt.zh: 想象你是1930年在帝国大厦工作的'sky boys'。这份工作最让你担心的是什么，又是什么让你自豪？把你的回答与书中描述的风险联系起来。

en: Mention the danger of working on narrow beams high above the street.
Note the pride in building something that became a world landmark.
zh: 提到在街道上方高处的窄梁上作业的危险。
指出参与建造一座世界地标带来的自豪感。
